from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Predicate-to-phrase mapping with natural language phrasing
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired on",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "is",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "flew on",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "included crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Find main subject (prefer person over mission/other entities)
    main_subject = None
    for subject in grouped:
        if any(t.predicate in ["occupation", "birthDate", "nationality"] for t in grouped[subject]):
            main_subject = subject
            break
    if main_subject is None:
        main_subject = list(grouped.keys())[0]

    # Build main clause for main subject
    main_subject_triples = grouped[main_subject]
    clauses = []

    # Sort triples for logical flow: birth info, occupation, mission, other info
    priority_order = ["birthDate", "birthPlace", "birthName", "nationality", "occupation",
                      "alternativeName", "mission", "selectedByNasa", "almaMater",
                      "timeInSpace", "status", "deathDate", "deathPlace", "awards", "award"]

    sorted_triples = sorted(main_subject_triples,
                           key=lambda t: priority_order.index(t.predicate) if t.predicate in priority_order else len(priority_order))

    for i, t in enumerate(sorted_triples):
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
        obj = t.object.strip('"')

        # Special handling for predicates
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
        elif t.predicate == "awards":
            if obj.isdigit():
                obj = f"{obj} awards"
        elif t.predicate == "status":
            obj = obj.lower()
        elif t.predicate == "ribbonAward":
            obj = obj.replace('.svg', '')
        elif t.predicate == "nationality":
            # Add "the" before country names
            if obj not in ["United States", "Canada", "Australia"]:
                obj = f"the {obj}"
        elif t.predicate == "birthPlace" or t.predicate == "deathPlace":
            # Add "the" before certain location types if needed
            if obj and not obj.startswith("the "):
                pass  # Keep original format for places

        # First clause doesn't need relative pronoun
        if i == 0:
            clauses.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
        else:
            # Use appropriate relative pronoun
            if t.predicate in ["birthDate", "birthPlace", "deathDate", "deathPlace", "nationality",
                              "occupation", "almaMater", "mission", "alternativeName", "awards", "status"]:
                clauses.append(f"who {verb} {obj}")
            else:
                clauses.append(f", {verb} {obj}")

    # Build base sentence with better flow
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}"
    else:
        # Connect clauses with better relative pronouns
        sentence = f"{main_subject}"
        for i, clause in enumerate(clauses):
            if clause.startswith("who"):
                sentence += f" {clause}"
            elif clause.startswith(","):
                if i == len(clauses) - 1:
                    sentence += f", and {clause[2:]}"
                else:
                    sentence += f" {clause}"
            else:
                # First clause - no relative pronoun
                if i == 0:
                    sentence += f" {clause}"
                else:
                    # Use appropriate connector for subsequent clauses
                    sentence += f", who {clause}"

    # Handle related subjects (like mission operators) - integrate into main sentence
    related_subjects = [s for s in grouped if s != main_subject]
    for subject in related_subjects:
        for t in grouped[subject]:
            verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
            obj = t.object.strip('"')

            if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
                obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
            elif t.predicate == "awards":
                if obj.isdigit():
                    obj = f"{obj} awards"
            elif t.predicate == "status":
                obj = obj.lower()

            # Add as relative clause with "which" or "where"
            if t.predicate in ["operator", "mission"]:
                sentence += f", which {verb} {obj}"
            else:
                # For other predicates, use appropriate relative pronoun
                if t.predicate in ["leader", "representative", "senators", "president"]:
                    sentence += f", whose {verb} {obj}"
                elif t.predicate in ["isPartOf", "part"]:
                    sentence += f", which {verb} {obj}"
                else:
                    sentence += f", where {subject} {verb} {obj}"

    # Capitalize first character and fix common grammatical issues
    if sentence:
        # Fix article issues (add "the" before country names and certain entities)
        sentence = sentence.replace(" of United States", " of the United States")
        sentence = sentence.replace(" in United States", " in the United States")
        sentence = sentence.replace(" citizen of United States", " citizen of the United States")
        sentence = sentence.replace(" leader | ", " leader | ")
        sentence = sentence.replace(" leader ", " leader ")

        # Ensure proper sentence ending
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
        if not sentence.endswith("."):
            sentence += "."
        return sentence
    return ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
